stories.title: Trump weighing all options on Iran's Kharg island
stories.description: The president said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.

stories.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
stories.description: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

stories.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
stories.description: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
stories.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

stories.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
stories.description: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.

stories.title: Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
stories.description: There are fears the Iranian proxy may attack shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic woes.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: Video shows US missile likely used in deadly strike on Iranian town, experts say
stories.description: The analysts identified a Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) in footage of the strike on the southern town of Lamerd.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Occupying Iran's Kharg island could be 'dangerous and risky' for US forces
stories.description: More American forces are reaching the Middle East. Before their arrival, the U.S. military had already built up the largest American force in the region in more than 20 years. stories.title: New Israeli defence budget 'another indication of breach between people and government'
stories.description: Israel’s parliament on March 30 passed its annual budget in a marathon overnight session, ensuring that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s government can complete its term until the fall. The opposition slammed the budget for increasing funding to Israel's ultra-Orthodox communi...

stories.title: Israel, US and Iran exchange airstrikes despite attempted talks
stories.description: Israel and Iran have continued their attacks on each other with the Israeli military striking targets in Tehran and The Islamic Republic's revolutionary guards said they hit an industrial complex in southern Israel. stories.title: What do we know about the failed bomb attack against Bank of America in Paris?
stories.description: French investigators suspect that the Iranian state is behind the attempted attack on Bank of America in Paris on March 29. Three suspects, all minors, area now in police custody, including those who attempted to detonate the explosive device. The Minister of the Interior suspect...

stories.title: Israel to expand operations in southern Lebanon
stories.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu says he has instructed an expansion of Israel's operations in southern Lebanon. This, as Israeli forces continue to target the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group. And with the Lebanese government now saying 1,200 people have been killed in the c...

stories.title: Trump wants to 'take oil' in Iran as 'tit-for-tat violence' continues
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has raised the idea of American forces seizing Iran’s Kharg Island, its main oil terminal in the Persian Gulf. This comes as US-Israeli strikes hit residential areas and other non-military targets in Tehran over the weekend. Iran has targeted industria...

stories.description: Oil surged over $115 a barrel this Monday as the war in Iran entered its fifth week. stories.title: Iraq risks Arab ties as Iran-aligned groups strike Gulf nations
stories.description: Joint Arab condemnation of cross-border attacks exposes Baghdad's failure to control armed proxies on its soil.

stories.title: Trump wants to invade Iran to seize oil, calls US objectors ‘stupid people’
stories.description: The Trump administration has deployed US Marines in preparation for the possible takeover of Iran's key oil export hub.

stories.title: ‘This is a nightmare scenario if it escalates further.’
stories.description: Elisabeth Kendall says the Houthi attack on Israel demonstrated a willingness to carry out their threats

stories.title: Did Israel miscalculate in launching the war on Iran?
stories.description: Like in other conflicts, Israel launched the war on Iran together with the US without a realistic political plan.

stories.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 31 of attacks

stories.title: ‘We’re killing them, bro’: Israelis correct Iranian online streamer
stories.description: Iranian musician thanks young Israeli women for ‘saving’ Iran through US-Israeli air strikes.

stories.title: IAEA says Iran’s Khondab heavy water reactor no longer operational
stories.description: The UN’s nuclear watchdog says Iran’s Khondab heavy water production plant is no longer operational.

stories.title: Russian tanker nears Cuba, defying US oil blockade
stories.description: HAVANA: A Russian oil tanker under US sanctions is scheduled to arrive in Cuba on Monday, challenging a de facto American fuel blockade of the energy-starved island, shipping data shows. The Anatoly Kolodkin, which is carrying 730,000 barrels of crude, was north of Haiti on Sunda...

stories.title: Australia to halve fuel tax to keep prices within reach of citizens amid Iran war
stories.description: Australia will halve a fuel tax to help motorists experiencing soaring petrol prices due to war in the Middle East, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Monday. Albanese announced the step after meeting with the leaders of Australia’s states and territories on Monday, agreeing a ...

stories.title: Global food waste crisis undermines climate and security, says UNEP official
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: The world wastes food on a staggering scale, as every year over one billion tonnes of edible food — nearly one-fifth of all food available to consumers — is thrown away, impacting both people and the environment, undermining food security and climate resilience, and co...

stories.title: Iran may withdraw from NPT, says diplomat
stories.description: The Islamic Republic of Iran has never sought and does not seek to develop nuclear weapons, Foreign Ministry Spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said

stories.description: Visitor locations have been damaged in 10 provinces across Iran

stories.description: Iran will continue with all its might to punish the aggressors, Seyyed Majid Ibn Reza said
